title: SubAgent Ontology Pipeline Review
method: Four read-only subAgent reviews covering CQs, baseline ontology, prompts, and pipeline.

findings.title: CQ artifacts are not strictly validated before generation or evaluation
evidence: src/aviation_agentic_ai/ontology/evaluation.py load_cqs coerces missing fields and skips malformed pages/questions.
src/aviation_agentic_ai/ontology/generation.py _load_cqs silently drops non-list page entries.
findings.impact: Broken CQ files can look like smaller valid benchmarks, weakening generation and evaluation evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add a strict CQ validator that fails on malformed pages, missing fields, empty entities, duplicate IDs, unknown ODP labels, and non-atomic records.

findings.title: CQ IDs are positional and not stable
evidence: Evaluation derives IDs as document:pX:qY from page and list position.
findings.impact: Insertion, deletion, deduplication, or reordering changes IDs and invalidates review traceability.
findings.recommendation: Add stable CQ IDs to the artifact and preserve them through generation, evaluation, sampling, and review reports.

findings.title: ODP labels are too fragmented for defensible stratification
evidence: The current CQ artifact has 334 CQs but 190 distinct odp_hint values.
Equivalent variants include Causal Relation, Causal Relation Pattern, and Causal Relation / Event Reification.
findings.impact: Sampling and reporting over raw ODP text creates noisy strata and weak coverage interpretation.
findings.recommendation: Normalize ODP hints into a controlled vocabulary and store both raw label and normalized odp_id.

findings.title: Lexical CQ coverage can overstate ontology support
evidence: reports/stages/ontology_evaluation.md reports 0.8744 entity mention coverage but this is name matching only.
match_entity uses normalized and substring matching rather than relation or answer support.
findings.impact: A matched term may exist while the ontology still lacks the relation, restriction, or axiom needed to answer the CQ.
findings.recommendation: Split CQ evaluation into lexical entity coverage, relation/property coverage, and answer-support coverage.

findings.title: Baseline verdict is too generous for ontology engineering quality
evidence: reports/stages/ontology_evaluation.md marks Valid TBox prototype as yes despite zero labels/comments and no owl:Ontology declaration.
findings.impact: The report may imply stronger ontology validity than the artifact currently supports.
findings.recommendation: Reframe the verdict as RDF-valid TBox extraction prototype until semantic checks, annotations, and CQ answerability improve.

findings.title: Baseline ontology conflates quantity kinds, units, assertions, states, and processes
evidence: Units such as Cl_PSI and Cl_Feet are modeled in the same broad quantity assertion area as physical quantities.
Physical quantities such as pressure, altitude, temperature, density, and viscosity are also represented near Cl_QuantityAssertion.
findings.impact: Reasoning and downstream extraction cannot reliably distinguish a quantity kind, measured value, unit, or assertion about a value.
findings.recommendation: Introduce clearer upper patterns for physical object, material, process/event, situation/state, force, quantity kind, quantity value, unit, and assertion.

findings.title: Several axioms appear semantically incoherent or likely false
evidence: Cl_PerfectlyDryAir is constrained with water vapor.
Cl_Pressure is constrained with both less-than and greater-than atmospheric pressure patterns.
Cl_Pressure has quantity some Cl_Pressure.
findings.impact: Syntax-valid Turtle can still encode misleading aviation knowledge and should not pass semantic quality review.
findings.recommendation: Add semantic smell checks for contradictory restrictions, self-quantity patterns, and aviation-domain plausibility flags.

findings.title: Core property semantics are weakened by broad owl:Thing domains and ranges
evidence: Core properties such as composedOf, hasComponent, hasQuantity, hasUnit, affects, and causes use broad domains/ranges.
findings.impact: Domain/range completeness is inflated while reasoning remains permissive and weak.
findings.recommendation: Track broad owl:Thing domain/range ratio and refine core property schemas toward domain-specific classes.

findings.title: SRD and TIP outputs have no enforceable schema
evidence: generation.py asks for JSON-only SRD but stores unchecked strings.
TIP output is also unchecked free text before being passed to the coder prompt.
findings.impact: Malformed JSON, missing CQ coverage, unsupported requirements, or ambiguous model output can flow into TTL generation.
findings.recommendation: Add Pydantic SRD and TIP models with required CQ IDs, evidence quotes, source pages, concepts, relations, constraints, and ambiguity flags.

findings.title: Turtle validation only checks syntax, not ontology intent
evidence: generation.py accepts any parseable Turtle candidate.
validation.py only parses RDF/Turtle.
findings.impact: The coder can drop prior terms, rename concepts, create ABox resources, omit domain/range, or invent unsupported terms and still pass.
findings.recommendation: Add post-generation gates for TBox-only output, namespace policy, no prior-term deletion, domain/range thresholds, CQ term coverage, and unsupported source claims.

findings.title: Complete-TTL rewrite is fragile as ontology size grows
evidence: The coder prompt asks the model to return the complete Turtle document for each page.
configs/ontology_generation.yaml caps max_tokens at 4096.
findings.impact: Later pages can truncate, compress, or drop accepted ontology content.
findings.recommendation: Prefer validated page-level Turtle additions or structured patches merged deterministically by code.

findings.title: Generated ontology is not evaluated by default
evidence: ontology generate writes data/ontology/generated/06_phak_ch4_0.generated.ttl.
ontology evaluate defaults to the baseline ontology unless --ontology-file is supplied.
findings.impact: A user can run generate then evaluate and accidentally evaluate the old baseline instead of the new generated ontology.
findings.recommendation: Document or implement a pipeline command that passes the same generated TTL through validate, report, and evaluate.

findings.title: Generation has no durable per-page artifacts or checkpoints
evidence: SRD, TIP, candidate TTL, and validation errors are kept in memory.
Only the final TTL is written after all pages finish.
findings.impact: Late-page failures lose accepted work and leave no audit trail for model outputs.
findings.recommendation: Persist per-page prompts, SRDs, TIPs, candidate TTLs, validation errors, accepted TTLs, and checkpoint state.

findings.title: LLM and run settings are not captured in outputs
evidence: Reports record paths, seed, sample size, and AI review flag, but not provider, model, base URL, config hash, source hash, CQ hash, or prompt version.
findings.impact: Generated artifacts and AI-review results are difficult to reproduce or audit.
findings.recommendation: Write a run manifest beside generated TTLs and evaluation reports with model/provider, config snapshot, hashes, prompt version, timestamp, and command args.

findings.title: AI review is enabled by default and fails all-or-nothing
evidence: ontology evaluate uses AI review unless --no-ai-review is passed.
A malformed AI response or transient API failure aborts evaluation before reports are written.
findings.impact: Evaluation can unexpectedly incur cost or disclose context, and partial results are not preserved on failures.
findings.recommendation: Make deterministic evaluation the default, require explicit --ai-review opt-in, and record per-CQ AI review errors instead of aborting the whole run.

actions.title: Add strict CQ validation and stable CQ identity
Reframe ontology verdict and add semantic quality gates
Introduce structured SRD/TIP schemas tied to CQ evidence
Write generation run manifests and per-page checkpoints
Make evaluation deterministic by default and align generated artifact evaluation
Refactor ontology upper patterns for quantities, units, states, and processes
